helpful: Provisioned full petition and BIO texts were decisive — the BIO's no-square-split and alternative-instrumentalities arguments drove the estimate down from the CFR-adjusted base.
Per-fee-class grant rates in statpack.json (paid vs IFP) gave a much better anchor than the whole-docket rate.
gaps: The prompt describes a per-Term 'Segment base rate by salience band' statpack section, but the committed statpack has no such section — no salience-band anchor was available.
No statpack cut for call-for-response (CFR) petitions; CFR is the strongest signal on this docket and had to be weighed from general knowledge rather than corpus data.
fedcourts query citation-overlap matching returned 0 rows for both SCOTUS citation queries; corpus rows appear to lack usable citation metadata for topical prior retrieval.
notes: Forward cell; ~2 corpus queries + 3 MCP searches, well under budget.
